questions.question: Why is await nextTick() needed before vi.advanceTimersByTime() when testing a debounce composable?
options: nextTick ensures the DOM is updated before time advances
Vue's reactivity queue must be flushed first so the watcher runs and registers the setTimeout
advanceTimersByTime requires a synchronization point before it can execute
nextTick resets the fake timer state to zero

questions.question: What must you call in afterEach to prevent fake timers from leaking into other tests?
When testing a composable that calls start() to begin setInterval directly, is await nextTick() needed before advancing time?
options: Yes — nextTick is always required before timer advancement
No — start() creates the setInterval synchronously without a reactive watcher involved
Yes — setInterval is async and needs a tick to register
No — vi.useFakeTimers() automatically flushes the reactivity queue
